title: Add option for ign override
body:
Users suggested we add the ability to merge a user's ignition file with
the ignition fie created by podman init.  You can now specify one or
more override files which will be added to the default ignition file.

Fixes: #11383

[NO NEW TESTS NEEDED]

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

<!--

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

A couple of concerns on this PR.  The first is that when ignition merges files, it is considered more of an add than an override, meaning the fields and values in the default ignition are not replaced.  I think this is probably acceptable but I have used the term override for the command line option.  I do not have a better term, but it really is not an override.

@baude: PR needs rebase.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

After more consideration of this and the reported issue, I am going to close this piece of work.  The problem with merging overrides is programmatically we do not know what is a replace vs addition.  For example, if you wanted to add a new directory and we did a replace merge, all other directory based stuff in the default ign file would be replaced.  It would work if we did an add merge.  However, something like ssh keys, most people would prefer an override over an add merge.

The certs issue is being handled by another PR.
